text: ## 📋 Summary Adds a client-side collapsible sidebar that persists preference in `localStorage` via `useSyncExternalStore` (with a server snapshot). The external-store wiring is mostly textbook-correct and a11y basics are in good shape, but the preference is **layout-affecting** and the SSR path always paints the wrong geometry for users who saved “collapsed,” which is a real product bug rather than an acceptable theme-style flash. ## 🔍 Findings ### 🟠 High - **`getServerSnapshot` always returns `"expanded"` while collapse changes layout (not just paint)** — `hard_app_sidebar.tsx` ~26–29, 32–33, 41 **Failure path:** User collapses sidebar → `localStorage["sidebar:collapsed"] = "1"` → full page load/SSR → server HTML is always the wide sidebar (`data-collapsed={false}`, nav labels mounted) → browser paints that layout → after hydration `useSyncExternalStore` reads `getSnapshot()` → `"collapsed"` → width/labels jump. That is CLS on every cold load for the cohort that used the feature; unlike color theme, this shifts page content and hit targets. **Fix:** Do not treat this like paint-only theme state: 1. Prefer a **cookie** (or other request-visible store) and pass the real initial value in (e.g. `defaultCollapsed` prop) so `getServerSnapshot` returns the user’s value and SSR HTML matches; or 2. Keep `localStorage` but add a **blocking boot script** in the root layout that reads the key and sets `data-sidebar-collapsed` on `` *before first paint*, with CSS width rules keyed off that attribute; optionally suppress width transitions until mounted so the post-hydration sync cannot animate the shift. ### 🟡 Medium - **Unguarded `localStorage` in the render path and in `toggle`** — ~20–24, 35–38 **Failure path:** `localStorage.getItem` / `setItem` throw (`SecurityError` in some iframe/privacy modes, quota errors, disabled storage) → `getSnapshot` throws during render → component hard-crashes; or `toggle` throws on click and the control silently dies. **Fix:** Wrap reads/writes in `try/catch` and fall back to `"expanded"` / no-op: ```ts function getSnapshot(): "collapsed" | "expanded" { try { return window.localStorage.getItem(KEY) === "1" ? "collapsed" : "expanded"; } catch { return "expanded"; } } ``` Same guard around `setItem` in `toggle`. ### 🟢 Low - **Collapse control still always mounts a text label** — ~49–50 vs ~66 Nav items hide labels when `collapsed`; the toggle does not. If collapsed width is icon-rail only, this label fights the layout (min-width/overflow) or relies on unshown CSS. Prefer `{!collapsed && …}` (icon + `aria-label` are enough). - **`storage` subscription is unfiltered** — ~15–18 Any other-tab `localStorage` write re-runs `getSnapshot`. Harmless (string snapshot is `Object.is`-stable) but noisy. Filter in the listener: only call `cb()` when `event.key === KEY || event.key === null`. - **No tests** for: server snapshot always `"expanded"`, client snapshot from `localStorage`, same-tab toggle via synthetic `storage` event, cross-tab updates, and `localStorage` throwing. ## ✅ Positives - Right API for a browser store: module-level `subscribe` / `getSnapshot` / `getServerSnapshot`, string snapshots (no infinite-loop object identities), and third-arg server snapshot so hydration markup can match. - Same-tab invalidation is handled (`setItem` then `dispatchEvent(new StorageEvent(...))`) in the correct order so the subscriber does not read a stale value. - Toggle button has `type="button"`, `aria-label`, and decorative icons are `aria-hidden`; nav has a proper landmark label; active link uses `aria-current`.
